Project_Description: Sound2Sleep addresses sleep disruption in children with epilepsy through an innovative closed-loop auditory stimulation device. With real-time detection of slow-wave brain oscillations, the technology delivers precise, phase-targeted bursts of pink noise to optimize the restorative properties of sleep. As a result, this intervention improves sleep quality and cognitive functioning in children with epilepsy. The take-home device offers families an accessible and non-invasive approach to managing sleep challenges, supporting children's daily performance and cognitive development.
Team_Members: Jaeyoung Kang, Vicki Li, Sean D’Mello, Kevin Xue
Favourite_Memory: Competing in the Norman Esch Pitch Competition. Working with venture capitalists to refine our pitch to have the best chance of winning.
Biggest_Challenges: Figuring out how to get our code to run with the EEG headset. It took a lot of experimentation and advice from advisors before we were able to get it to work.
Takeaway: How to work with a team and coordinate efforts so that everyone’s parts can be combined in the final product.
Advice: Use your co-ops as an opportunity to explore potential capstone projects. Ask your mentors if they have any ideas for a potential project.
